module.name: tierlist
module.description: A tier list (S/A/B/… rows) with coloured tier labels and items on each row. One tier per section: first line = tier name, the rest = its items (Markdown).
module.aiSpec:
A TIER LIST — ranked rows (S, A, B, C …) each with a coloured label box and a set of items. Block module: split the body with `<!-- @ -->`, one tier per section. The FIRST line of a section is the tier name; everything after is the tier's items (Markdown — write a `- ` list, or comma / newline separated items). Tiers are coloured top→bottom (S = red/gold, down to blue/grey). Params: `color` — one colour for ALL tier labels instead of the per-rank palette (default empty = auto palette). Good for rankings, prioritisation, quick verdicts. For a simple comparison grid use a Markdown table.
<!-- @tierlist --> S - Rust - Go <!-- @ --> A - Python - TypeScript <!-- @end -->

param.name: color
param.label: Override label color (optional)
param.desc: Single colour for every tier label; empty = automatic per-rank palette (red, orange, yellow, ...).

snippet.label: Tier list (S/A/B)
snippet.text: <!-- @tierlist --> S Must-have, high impact <!-- @ --> A Important <!-- @ --> B Nice to have <!-- @end -->
snippet.description: Ranked tiers; each section = tier name then its items.
